keyMyths.title: The Osiris Myth
keyMyths.description: Osiris, a benevolent king, is murdered by his jealous brother Set, dismembered, and scattered across Egypt. Isis, his wife, collects the pieces, resurrects him, and conceives Horus, ensuring his eventual revenge and Osiris's role as ruler of the underworld.

keyMyths.title: The Myth of Tammuz/Dumuzi
keyMyths.description: Tammuz, the Mesopotamian god of vegetation, dies and descends to the underworld, causing the land to become barren. His consort, Ishtar/Inanna, descends to rescue him, leading to a cycle of his death and return that mirrors the changing seasons.

keyMyths.title: The Mysteries of Attis
keyMyths.description: Attis, the consort of Cybele, castrates himself and dies, with varying accounts of his death. Cybele mourns him, and through her power, he is resurrected, symbolizing rebirth and the cyclical nature of life and death. Celebrations involved ritualistic mourning and ecstatic rites.

keyMyths.title: The Myth of Persephone
keyMyths.description: Persephone, daughter of Demeter, is abducted by Hades to be his queen in the underworld. Demeter's grief causes a famine on Earth, and Zeus intervenes, decreeing that Persephone must spend part of the year in the underworld and part on Earth, explaining the seasons.

keyMyths.title: The Story of Jesus Christ
keyMyths.description: Jesus, the central figure of Christianity, is crucified and dies, then is resurrected from the dead three days later. This event is foundational to Christian belief, representing the triumph over sin and death and offering salvation to believers.

sources.name: Frazer, James George. *The Golden Bough: A Study in Magic and Religion*.
sources.description: A comprehensive, though now somewhat outdated, comparative study of mythology and religion that extensively discusses dying and rising gods.
sources.title: Frazer, James George. *The Golden Bough: A Study in Magic and Religion*.

sources.name: Eliade, Mircea. *The Sacred and the Profane: The Nature of Religion*.
sources.description: Explores the concept of the sacred and its manifestation in various cultures, including the cyclical nature of life and death as seen in dying and rising deities.
sources.title: Eliade, Mircea. *The Sacred and the Profane: The Nature of Religion*.

sources.name: Tryggve Mettinger, *The Riddle of Resurrection: 'Dying and Rising Gods' in the Ancient Near East*.
sources.description: A critical examination of the dying and rising god motif in ancient Near Eastern religions, challenging some of Frazer's claims.
sources.title: Tryggve Mettinger, *The Riddle of Resurrection: 'Dying and Rising Gods' in the Ancient Near East*.

sources.name: Burkert, Walter. *Ancient Mystery Cults*.
sources.description: Provides insights into the mystery cults of the ancient world, many of which centered around dying and rising gods and offered initiates the promise of rebirth.
sources.title: Burkert, Walter. *Ancient Mystery Cults*.

sources.name: Plutarch. *Isis and Osiris*.
sources.description: A primary source offering a detailed account of the Egyptian myth of Osiris, including his death, dismemberment, and resurrection.
sources.title: Plutarch. *Isis and Osiris*.
